content.problemStatement:
You are given a **binary** string AAA of length NNN.

Let F(i,j)F(i,j)F(i,j) denote the length of **longest non-decreasing** subsequence of the substring [Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,Aj][A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_j][Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Aj‚Äã].

Find the value ‚àëi=1N‚àëj=iNF(i,j)\sum_{i=1}^{N} \sum_{j=i}^{N}F(i,j)‚àëi=1N‚Äã‚àëj=iN‚ÄãF(i,j).


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the binary string AAA.
The second line of each test case contains AAA, a binary string of size NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required sum.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

F(1,1)=1F(1,1) = 1F(1,1)=1
F(1,2)=2F(1,2) = 2F(1,2)=2, since the substring 111111 is non-decreasing.
F(2,2)=1F(2,2) = 1F(2,2)=1

Thus, sum of all F(i,j)F(i,j)F(i,j) is 444.
Test case 222:

F(1,1)=1F(1,1) = 1F(1,1)=1
F(1,2)=1F(1,2) = 1F(1,2)=1, since the longest non-decreasing subsequence is length 111. Both longest non-decreasing subsequences, 000 and 111, are of length 111.
F(2,2)=1F(2,2) = 1F(2,2)=1

Thus, sum of all F(i,j)F(i,j)F(i,j) is 333.
